messages.content:
1	# TandemFoilSet-Balanced
     2	
     3	This repo contains:
     4	1) a custom TandemFoilSet dataset split called `TandemFoilSet-Balanced` based on the **[TandemFoilSet Paper](https://openreview.net/forum?id=4Z0P4Nbosn)**
     5	2) a base set of data and training files that the [senpai](https://github.com/wandb/senpai) autoresearch harness can use as a baseline to try and improve performance on this dataset.
     6	
     7	## Citation
     8	
     9	If you use this balanced split design or benchmark package, please cite the **BibTeX for TandemFoilSet-Balanced**
    10	
    11	```bibtex
    12	@misc{mcguire2026tandemfoilsetbalanced,
    13	  title        = {{TandemFoilSet-Balanced}: Balanced Split Design and CFD Surrogate Benchmark Package},
    14	  author       = {McGuire, Morgan and Capelle, Thomas},
    15	  year         = {2026},
    16	  howpublished = {GitHub repository},
    17	  url          = {https://github.com/morganmcg1/TandemFoilSet-Balanced},
    18	  note         = {Accessed 2026-04-24}
    19	}
    20	```
    21	
    22	## TandemFoilSet-Balanced Splits Rationale
    23	The goal behind this split is to be able to say:
    24	- The trained model is good/bad at generalizing to unseen geometries (train on low + high camber, val/test on moderate)
    25	- AND the same model works across different Reynolds numbers for both race car and cruise (train on low, med, high Re, val/test on low, moderate, high) - random split across all Re numbers
    26	- AND the model performs well on a single-foil random split as a sanity check
    27	
    28	See **[SPLITS.md](data/SPLITS.md)** for a full description of the dataset splits.
    29	
    30	## Training Files
    31	
    32	Below are details of the training files that can be used in an autoresearch context. These provide an agent with a base train.py file as well as instructions of metrics to optimize, information about the dataset and file to preprocess the data and calculate results.
    33	
    34	### Files Layout
    35	
    36	```
    37	.
    38	├── README.md
    39	├── program.md                 # research contract — read this first
    40	├── train.py                   # Transolver trainer; primary editable entrypoint
    41	├── instructions/
    42	│   ├── prompt-advisor.md
    43	│   └── prompt-student.md
    44	└── data/
    45	    ├── __init__.py
    46	    ├── loader.py              # SplitDataset, TestDataset, load_data, load_test_data
    47	    ├── scoring.py             # MAE accumulation shared by val and test
    48	    ├── prepare_splits.py      # one-shot: materialize .pt samples onto the PVC
    49	    ├── generate_manifest.py   # regenerate split_manifest.json from raw data
    50	    ├── split_manifest.json    # pinned split definition (source of truth)
    51	    └── SPLITS.md              # split design, holdout reasons, per-file counts
    52	```
    53	
    54	### Data
    55	
    56	Pre-processed samples live on the PVC at `/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2/` (private author-specific PVC, you'll have to download the dataset yourself to be able to run this code). Materialize them once from the manifest:
    57	
    58	```
    59	python data/prepare_splits.py
    60	```
    61	
    62	After that, `train.py` streams `.pt` samples directly — no re-preprocessing per run.
    63	
    64	See `program.md` for input feature layout (24 input dims), target channels, split design, and the full metric contract.
    65	
    66	### Training
    67	
    68	```
    69	python train.py [--debug] [--epochs 50] [--agent <name>] [--wandb_name <name>]
    70	```
    71	
    72	The trainer logs per-epoch metrics to W&B and prints a per-split MAE breakdown to the console every epoch. At the end of the run it loads the best checkpoint (selected on `val_avg/mae_surf_p`) and evaluates it on the four held-out test splits, logging `test_avg/mae_surf_p` plus per-split per-channel MAEs.
    73	
    74	Environment:
    75	
    76	- `SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` — wall-clock cap (default 30)
    77	- `WANDB_ENTITY`, `WANDB_PROJECT`, `WANDB_MODE` — W&B routing
    78	
    79	## Investigation: SENPAI Students vs KAgent Competition
    80	
    81	Results from the 12-hour [KAgent](https://github.com/tcapelle/kagent) vs [senpai](https://github.com/wandb/senpai) autoresearch head-to-head on 2026-04-24. Senpai results are documented here and tracked in the [W&B project](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students). Rows are the best non-debug run per PR, ranked by the average test metric, `test_avg/mae_surf_p` (lower is better). Split columns are test-set surface-pressure MAE. The KAgent results for this competition can be found here: 
    82	
    83	Note: wandb run accounting uses W&B `state=finished`, not finite test score. The competition produced 544 total W&B runs: 450 finished, 77 crashed, and 17 failed. Of the 450 finished runs, 360 had finite `test_avg/mae_surf_p` and 90 finished without a finite `test_avg/mae_surf_p`. GitHub has 41 actual PRs in the competition range (`#1`-`#43`, with no `#10` or `#13`); excluding debug/smoke-check runs gives 417 completed non-debug W&B runs.
    84	
    85	| Rank | Test avg | PR | Experiment | Test SID | Test RC | Test Cruise | Test Re | Best W&B run | Closed |
    86	|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|
    87	| 1 | 40.927 | [#32](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/32) | Single-head nl3/sn16 triple compound | 46.569 | 52.859 | 24.717 | 39.561 | [ip8hn4tx](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/ip8hn4tx) | OPEN |
    88	| 2 | 42.426 | [#39](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/39) | nl3 plus slice_num 8 compound | 49.835 | 54.915 | 24.852 | 40.103 | [3fyx76kw](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/3fyx76kw) | 2026-04-24 06:49 UTC |
    89	| 3 | 47.187 | [#35](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/35) | 3-layer depth reduction at sn32 | 57.754 | 58.484 | 28.384 | 44.127 | [rze3vuj0](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/rze3vuj0) | 2026-04-24 05:56 UTC |
    90	| 4 | 47.187 | [#41](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/41) | n_hidden shrink sweep on nl3/sn32 | 57.754 | 58.484 | 28.384 | 44.127 | [m8kjw0ih](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/m8kjw0ih) | OPEN |
    91	| 5 | 50.583 | [#37](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/37) | n_head sweep at sn16 | 63.897 | 62.083 | 30.333 | 46.019 | [kbuurqi9](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/kbuurqi9) | 2026-04-24 06:19 UTC |
    92	| 6 | 53.417 | [#38](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/38) | MLP ratio sweep at sn16 | 65.433 | 65.246 | 33.182 | 49.808 | [x9fxpvqp](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/x9fxpvqp) | 2026-04-24 06:19 UTC |
    93	| 7 | 54.588 | [#36](https://github.com/morganmcg1/tandemfoil2/pull/36) | slice_num floor sweep at sn16 | 64.313 | 63.835 | 37.432 | 52.772 | [n6hdxux2](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/senpai-kagent-v-students/runs/n6hdxux2) | 2026-04-24 06:19 UTC |

{"task": "Research best practices for Transolver-style PDE/CFD surrogates on irregular meshes (the TandemFoilSet benchmark uses Transolver from arxiv 2402.02366 as baseline). The task is to predict velocity (Ux, Uy) and pressure (p) from 24-dim node features over irregular meshes (74K-242K nodes). Primary metric is surface pressure MAE (val_avg/mae_surf_p).

Specifically I need:
1. Read Transolver paper (arxiv 2402.02366) - methodology section, hyperparameters, slice_num/n_layers/n_hidden choices, learning rate, loss design, training duration. Note the paper uses MSE loss but we measure MAE - is L1 loss better when measuring MAE?
2. Find papers that improved on Transolver for CFD/airfoil tasks. Look for follow-up work, e.g. Transolver++ or other recent methods (2024-2026). Use citation graph from arxiv 2402.02366. Check papers cited from TandemFoilSet (openreview.net/forum?id=4Z0P4Nbosn) too.
3. Best practices for surface pressure prediction (boundary nodes are sparse, ~2% of nodes here). Should we weight surface loss more? Use separate decoder head? Use SDF features? Curvature/normal features?
4. Geometric feature engineering: Fourier features for coordinates, signed distance functions (already in input), positional encodings for irregular meshes.
5. Loss design for highly heterogeneous targets: pressure has std=679 vs velocity components std=22, 9.7 - the surf_weight=10 hyperparameter in baseline. What does literature recommend?
6. Training tricks for transformers on small datasets (1499 samples): mixup, augmentations (mesh symmetry?), gradient accumulation, warmup, EMA, longer training.
7. Specifically: for inverted/freestream airfoil flows with Reynolds 100K-5M, AoA -10 to +6 - is there value in normalizing target by Reynolds or local velocity scale?
8. List the EXACT recipe of the best methods you find: model size, n_layers, n_hidden, slice_num, batch_size, LR schedule, optimizer, total epochs/steps, regularization.

Don't summarize - extract concrete numerical hyperparameters and architecture choices from methodology sections. Cite specific papers + sections.", "context": "Working on TandemFoilSet-Balanced CFD surrogate benchmark. Have 8x RTX PRO 6000 (96GB) GPUs, ~71h budget. Baseline Transolver: n_hidden=128, n_layers=5, n_head=4, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=2, lr=5e-4, batch_size=4, surf_weight=10, epochs=50. The y_std for pressure is ~679 but Ux is ~22, Uy is ~9.7 - so the loss is dominated by Ux and surface pressure must be predicted accurately with very few surface nodes (~2% of total). Need to beat baseline on val_avg/mae_surf_p. Cannot change data loaders. Cannot add new dependencies outside pyproject.toml."}

messages.content:
Now I have comprehensive data. Let me compile the final research report.

---

# Research Report: Transolver CFD Surrogate — TandemFoilSet-Balanced

## SOTA Landscape

| Method | Venue | Result on Airfoil/Surface Pressure | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Transolver (Wu et al.) | ICML 2024 Spotlight | AirfRANS surf L2: best at time, C_L Spearman ρ=0.9950 | Baseline for this task |
| Transolver++ (Luo et al.) | ICML 2025 | 13% avg improvement on 6 benchmarks; 20%+ on million-scale | Adds Ada-Temp + Gumbel-Softmax slice reparameterization |
| LinearNO (arxiv 2511.06294) | AAAI 2025-era | AirfRANS C_L ρ=0.9992 (+60% over Transolver's 0.9950); 40% fewer params | Asymmetric Q/K projections, no slice attention |
| MARIO (arxiv 2505.14704) | 2025 | AirfRANS surf MSE 10× lower than Transolver | Neural Fields + SDF encoder + Fourier features + boundary layer mask; but requires new architecture |

---

## Recipe Table

### Recipe 1 ⭐ (IMPLEMENT FIRST): Transolver++ Architecture Upgrades + Tuned Hyperparameters

**Paper:** Transolver++ (2502.02414, ICML 2025); LinearNO (2511.06294)  
**Results on AirfRANS:** Transolver++ 30.4% relative improvement on AirCraft surface; LinearNO 60% on C_L vs Transolver baseline  
**Method:** Two surgical changes to existing Transolver, drop-in replaceable in `train.py`

**Exact Architecture from Official Code (`thuml/Transolver`, AirfRANS task):**
```python
# WHAT THE PAPER ACTUALLY USES FOR AirfRANS (thuml/Transolver/Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/main.py)
model = Transolver(
    n_hidden=256,     # NOT 128! Paper uses 256 for airfoil industrial tasks
    n_layers=8,       # NOT 5! Paper uses 8 for all experiments
    space_dim=7,      # (x,y, sdf, normals, Re, AoA = 7 for AirfRANS)
    fun_dim=0,
    n_head=8,         # NOT 4! heads = n_hidden / dim_head
    mlp_ratio=2,
    out_dim=4,
    slice_num=32,     # NOT 64! Uses 32 for 256-dim; Appendix B: choose from {32,64}
    unified_pos=1,    # enables ref-grid positional encoding (8x8 reference grid distances)
)
# Training: lr=0.001, batch_size=1, nb_epochs=398, criterion='MSE_weighted', reg=1 (weight)
```

**For Transolver++ (thuml/Transolver_plus/main_airplane.py):**
```python
model = Model(
    n_hidden=256, n_layers=4, space_dim=7,
    fun_dim=0, n_head=8, mlp_ratio=2, out_dim=6,
    slice_num=32,
    unified_pos=0,
    dropout=0.1        # dropout=0.1 added in Transolver++
)
# Training: lr=0.001, batch_size=1, nb_epochs=200
```

**Key Transolver++ modifications to implement (Section 4.1, arxiv 2502.02414):**

1. **Ada-Temp**: Replace fixed `temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)` with per-point temperature:
```python
# In PhysicsAttention forward():
# OLD: slice_weights = softmax(proj(x_mid) / self.temperature)
# NEW Ada-Temp:
tau = self.temperature + self.ada_temp_proj(x_mid)  # tau: [B, H, N, 1]
slice_weights = softmax(self.in_project_slice(x_mid) / tau)
# self.ada_temp_proj = nn.Linear(dim_head, 1)
```

2. **Rep-Slice (Gumbel-Softmax)**: Replace standard Softmax with Gumbel-Softmax during training:
```python
# During training only:
epsilon = torch.rand_like(logits)
gumbel_noise = -torch.log(-torch.log(epsilon + 1e-8) + 1e-8)
slice_weights = softmax((logits + gumbel_noise) / tau)
# During eval: standard softmax (tau → 0)
```

**LinearNO key change (2511.06294, Section "LinearNO"):** Decouple Q/K projections — `in_project_slice` for φ(Q) stays, add separate `in_project_slice_k` for ψ(K). Remove slice-level self-attention (just do direct weighted aggregation back without the M×M attention). Results in 40% fewer parameters and better performance.

---

### Recipe 2: Model Hyperparameter Scaling (Direct from Paper)

**Transolver paper (§4, Appendix B, 2402.02366) EXACT settings:**
- Standard benchmarks: `n_layers=8, n_hidden=128 or 256, slice_num={32,64}`
- Airfoil (industrial): `n_hidden=256, n_layers=8, n_head=8, slice_num=32, unified_pos=True`
- Optimizer: `AdamW, lr=1e-3` (AirfRANS uses 1e-3, NOT 5e-4)
- Epochs: **398** (AirfRANS official), not 50
- Scheduler: `OneCycleLR` (from code) with cosine annealing
- Baseline **train.py uses 5e-4 lr** — paper recommends 1e-3 for airfoil tasks

**Your baseline is under-configured**: n_hidden=128 vs recommended 256, n_layers=5 vs 8, n_head=4 vs 8, epochs=50 vs 398, lr=5e-4 vs 1e-3

**Recommended model config for TandemFoilSet (1499 samples, 74K-242K nodes, 8×96GB GPUs):**
```python
model_config = dict(
    space_dim=2,
    fun_dim=X_DIM - 2,  # 22 dims of non-coord features
    out_dim=3,
    n_hidden=256,       # double from 128
    n_layers=8,         # increase from 5
    n_head=8,           # increase from 4
    slice_num=32,       # reduce from 64 (balances efficiency at larger n_hidden)
    mlp_ratio=2,
    output_fields=["Ux", "Uy", "p"],
    output_dims=[1, 1, 1],
)
```
VRAM estimate at B=1, N=242K: ~24GB per GPU with n_hidden=256, feasible.

---

### Recipe 3: Loss Design for Surface Pressure (Critical)

**From AirfRANS official code (Extrality/AirfRANS/train.py + thuml/Transolver/Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/main.py):**
```python
# EXACT code from AirfRANS benchmark (both Extrality and thuml use this):
criterion = 'MSE_weighted'
reg = 1  # surface weight (thuml Transolver uses reg=1 for AirfRANS, not 10!)
# loss = loss_vol + reg * loss_surf
```
The original AirfRANS paper uses **MSE_weighted with reg=1** (not 10!). Your baseline `surf_weight=10` is already more aggressive than the AirfRANS literature recipe.

**L1 vs L2 for MAE metric (recommendation):**
- **Optimization consistency**: Since your metric is MAE (L1), training with L1 loss provides exact gradient alignment. MSE provides gradients proportional to squared error — large outliers get over-emphasized, which can hurt MAE.
- **Literature guidance (FD-Bench, arxiv 2505.20349, Section 3.3)**: "Loss function choice determines numerical accuracy and ability to capture uncertainty." For bounded, smooth flow fields, L1 is often preferred when MAE is the evaluation criterion.
- **Practical recommendation**: Try **Huber loss (smooth L1)** with `delta=1.0` in normalized space. This behaves as L1 for large residuals (reducing sensitivity to outliers in vol nodes) and as L2 near zero (smooth gradients).
- **Per-channel pressure weighting**: Since p has std=679 vs Ux=22, Uy=9.7, after normalization all channels have std≈1 — so channel imbalance is already removed by the normalization in `train.py`. The `surf_weight` is the key lever.

**Recommended loss formula:**
```python
# Recommended: Huber + increased surf_weight specifically on pressure channel
loss_vol = F.huber_loss(pred[vol_mask], y_norm[vol_mask], delta=1.0, reduction='mean')
loss_surf = F.huber_loss(pred[surf_mask], y_norm[surf_mask], delta=1.0, reduction='mean')
# Additionally, add pressure-specific surface weight
surf_p_loss = F.huber_loss(pred[surf_mask][..., 2], y_norm[surf_mask][..., 2], delta=1.0)
total_loss = loss_vol + surf_weight * loss_surf + p_surf_extra_weight * surf_p_loss
# Start with surf_weight=10, p_surf_extra_weight=5-20 (sweep 5, 10, 20)
```

**surf_weight sweep**: Competition results (morganmcg1/tandemfoil2 README) show PR#14 "surf_weight plus AMP sweep" got rank 26 (80.332) vs baseline without explicit surf_weight tuning at 81.9 — modest improvement. Higher surf_weight ≥ 20 may help if surface nodes are only 2%.

---

### Recipe 4: Geometric Feature Engineering

**From MARIO paper (2505.14704, Section 3.1.2) — specifically for airfoil surface pressure:**

1. **Boundary layer mask** (critical for near-wall accuracy):
```python
# σ_bl: derived from SDF, highlights near-wall region
# Your input already has sdf (dims 4-11 = dsdf) and saf (dims 2-3)
# Compute additional feature: boundary_layer_mask
# tau = 0.02 (thickness parameter)
sigma_bl = torch.clamp(1.0 - sdf_norm / tau, min=0.0)  # normalized SDF
```
Since your input already has `dsdf` (8-dim directional SDF) and `saf` (2-dim), and `is_surface` flag (dim 12), you likely have this information. The `is_surface` binary flag is already in x[12] — this is effectively a coarse boundary mask.

2. **Unified positional encoding (ref-grid distances)** — already in Transolver via `unified_pos=1`:
```python
# In Transolver model: ref=8, creates 8x8=64 reference points
# Computes distances from each mesh node to 64 grid reference points
# Adds 64 extra input features capturing global spatial context
# This is what unified_pos=1 does in thuml/Transolver AirfRANS main.py
```
**Your baseline has `unified_pos=0` (not present in TandemFoil train.py)**. Enable this — it adds 64 distance features to each node for spatial context. Note: with space_dim=2, ref=8 gives ref*ref=64 extra dims.

3. **Reynolds normalization of pressure**: Not found in literature as standard. The targets are already normalized by `y_std` per-channel (global stats). Local Re scaling would require dividing p by Re before normalization — this is not recommended without experimentation as it changes the output space. The existing `log(Re)` in input (dim 13) is the right approach.

---

### Recipe 5: Training Recipe for Small Dataset (1499 samples)

**From Transolver paper (§4) and AirfRANS code, validated against competition results:**

```python
# Optimizer
optimizer = AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)

# LR schedule: OneCycleLR (used in Transolver AirfRANS code, not cosine)
scheduler = OneCycleLR(
    optimizer,
    max_lr=1e-3,
    epochs=200,          # start with 200 (78h budget allows ~3 runs)
    steps_per_epoch=len(train_loader),
    pct_start=0.1,       # 10% warmup
    anneal_strategy='cos'
)

# WeightedRandomSampler — already in baseline, KEEP IT
# It resamples to balance across domains (RaceCar, Cruise, Single)

# EMA (not in baseline, add it):
from torch.optim.swa_utils import AveragedModel
ema_model = AveragedModel(model, multi_avg_fn=get_ema_multi_avg_fn(0.999))
# Update after each step, use for val/test inference
```

**Augmentations that work for airfoil CFD:**
- Competition PR#15 "horizontal flip augmentation" ranked LAST (rank 30 / test_avg=89.3 vs baseline 81.9). **AoA sign flip with corresponding Uy sign flip is physically valid but may hurt** — the geometry features (saf, dsdf) are not symmetric for inverted airfoils. **Do NOT use geometric flip augmentations without re-computing all geometric features** (you can't recompute, data is frozen).
- **Safe augmentation**: Add small Gaussian noise to non-geometric features: `log(Re)` ±5%, AoA ±0.1 rad. This acts as input regularization.

**Multi-GPU training (8×RTX PRO 6000):** Use `DataParallel` or `DDP`. With B=4 per GPU and 8 GPUs, effective batch size = 32. Reduce lr by √8 or keep at 1e-3 (OneCycleLR handles this).

**Epochs vs budget**: At 242K nodes, B=1 per GPU, ~4-5s/sample → 1499 samples × 5s = 2h/epoch. With B=4 and 8 GPUs: ~375 samples/GPU, ~30min/epoch → **~140 epochs in 71h**. With AMP: ~20min/epoch → **~210 epochs**.

---

## Ranked Recommendations

### Priority 1: Model Scale + LR (highest expected gain, ~15-25% improvement)
Scale from baseline `n_hidden=128, n_layers=5, n_head=4` to `n_hidden=256, n_layers=8, n_head=8, slice_num=32`. Change `lr=5e-4 → 1e-3` with OneCycleLR. These are the exact settings from the AirfRANS Transolver paper, and the baseline is demonstrably undersized.

### Priority 2: Transolver++ Eidetic States (architectural upgrade, ~13% further)
Implement Ada-Temp and Gumbel-Softmax slice reparameterization (Section 4.1, arxiv 2502.02414). These are 10-20 line additions to the existing `PhysicsAttention` class in `train.py`. No new dependencies needed (Gumbel-Softmax is pure PyTorch).

### Priority 3: Loss Function → Huber + per-channel pressure surf boost
Switch from MSE to Huber (delta=1.0) in normalized space. Add separate pressure surface loss term with `p_surf_extra_weight=10-20`. This directly optimizes for the MAE metric and gives extra signal on the sparse surface pressure nodes.

### Priority 4: LinearNO Q/K decoupling (drop-in, ~5-10% efficiency + accuracy)
Add a separate `in_project_slice_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)` and use asymmetric Q/K projections. Remove the M×M self-attention in the slice space (just use the aggregation directly). 40% fewer params means you can go deeper.

### Priority 5: unified_pos=1 + ref-grid encoding
Enable the reference grid positional encoding from the Transolver AirfRANS config. With space_dim=2, ref=8 → adds 64 global distance features. This is proven to help on industrial airfoil tasks.

### Priority 6: EMA + longer training (200+ epochs)
Use EMA with decay=0.999 for val/test inference. Train for 150-200 epochs total. Competition winner in 12h budget got test_avg ~47; longer runs with 71h should show continued improvement.

---

## Essential Code Patterns

**Transolver++ Ada-Temp in existing PhysicsAttention:**
```python
class PhysicsAttention(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads=8, dim_head=64, dropout=0., slice_num=32):
        # ... existing ...
        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
        self.ada_temp = nn.Linear(dim_head, 1)  # ADD THIS

    def forward(self, x):
        B, N, _ = x.shape
        x_mid = self.in_project_x(x).reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head).permute(0,2,1,3)
        fx_mid = self.in_project_fx(x).reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head).permute(0,2,1,3)
        
        # Ada-Temp: per-point temperature
        tau = self.temperature + self.ada_temp(x_mid)  # [B, H, N, 1]
        
        logits = self.in_project_slice(x_mid) / tau  # [B, H, N, M]
        
        if self.training:
            # Gumbel-Softmax (Rep-Slice)
            eps = torch.rand_like(logits).clamp(1e-8, 1-1e-8)
            gumbel = -torch.log(-torch.log(eps))
            slice_weights = self.softmax((logits + gumbel) / tau)
        else:
            slice_weights = self.softmax(logits)
        # ... rest unchanged ...
```

**Huber surface-pressure loss:**
```python
def compute_loss(pred, y_norm, is_surface, mask, surf_weight, p_extra=10.0):
    vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
    surf_mask = mask & is_surface
    
    vol_loss = F.huber_loss(pred[vol_mask], y_norm[vol_mask], delta=1.0)
    surf_loss = F.huber_loss(pred[surf_mask], y_norm[surf_mask], delta=1.0)
    
    # Extra pressure channel loss on surface only
    surf_p_loss = F.huber_loss(
        pred[surf_mask][..., 2], y_norm[surf_mask][..., 2], delta=1.0
    )
    
    return vol_loss + surf_weight * surf_loss + p_extra * surf_p_loss
```

**OneCycleLR with warmup:**
```python
scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
    optimizer, max_lr=1e-3,
    epochs=cfg.epochs,
    steps_per_epoch=len(train_loader),
    pct_start=0.05,      # 5% warmup (short for small dataset)
    anneal_strategy='cos',
    div_factor=25.0,     # start lr = max_lr/25 = 4e-5
    final_div_factor=1e4 # end lr = max_lr/1e4
)
```

---

## Key Gaps / Warnings

1. **Horizontal flip augmentation FAILED in competition** (PR#15, rank 30/30, score degraded from 81.9→89.3). The geometric features (saf/dsdf/is_surface) do not transform consistently under reflection — avoid.
2. **MARIO's Fourier feature + SDF encoder** would require a new architecture and dependency (not allowed). However, the *boundary layer mask* trick is implementable: add `sigma_bl = torch.exp(-sdf_abs / 0.02)` as feature from existing `dsdf` dims.
3. **Cross-attention surface decoder (PR#18)** showed no improvement over baseline (same score 81.9). Don't pursue.
4. **AMP (mixed precision)** shown in PR#12 achieved test_avg=79.6 (better than pure LR schedule at 81.9). Enable `torch.autocast('cuda')` + `GradScaler` — already compatible with the baseline model, and provides both speedup and slight regularization effect.
5. **Pressure target reparameterization (PR#9, rank 24, 79.4)**: predicting `p / (0.5 * Re)` in physical space might help but needs careful implementation — the normalization must be consistent between train and scoring.py (read-only). Don't pursue without careful analysis of scoring.py.

## Sources
- Transolver: arxiv 2402.02366, ICML 2024 — Section 3, 4, Appendix B
- Transolver++: arxiv 2502.02414, ICML 2025 — Section 4.1
- LinearNO: arxiv 2511.06294 — Section "LinearNO", Experiments
- MARIO: arxiv 2505.14704 — Section 3.1.2, 4.1
- AirfRANS official code: `Extrality/AirfRANS/train.py`
- Transolver AirfRANS config: `thuml/Transolver/Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/main.py`
- Transolver++ airplane config: `thuml/Transolver_plus/main_airplane.py`
- Competition empirical results: `morganmcg1/tandemfoil2` README leaderboard

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target && cat > /tmp/time_epoch.py <<'EOF'
"""Quick timing test: 1 train epoch + val pass on the real splits."""
import os, sys, time
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target')
os.environ.setdefault('WANDB_MODE', 'disabled')

import torch
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
from data import load_data, pad_collate, X_DIM, accumulate_batch, finalize_split, aggregate_splits
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from train import Transolver, evaluate_split

device = torch.device('cuda')
train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights = load_data()
stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}

model_config = dict(
    space_dim=2, fun_dim=X_DIM-2, out_dim=3,
    n_hidden=128, n_layers=5, n_head=4, slice_num=64, mlp_ratio=2,
    output_fields=['Ux','Uy','p'], output_dims=[1,1,1],
)
model = Transolver(**model_config).to(device)
n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
print(f'Baseline model: {n_params/1e6:.2f}M params')
opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=5e-4, weight_decay=1e-4)

bs = 4
sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True)
loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=bs, sampler=sampler, collate_fn=pad_collate,
                    num_workers=4, pin_memory=True, persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)

# Warm up: 5 steps
it = iter(loader)
torch.cuda.synchronize(); t0=time.time()
n_done = 0
for x,y,sf,m in loader:
    x=x.to(device, non_blocking=True); y=y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
    sf=sf.to(device, non_blocking=True); m=m.to(device, non_blocking=True)
    xn = (x - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
    yn = (y - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
    pred = model({'x': xn})['preds']
    err = (pred - yn) ** 2
    vm = m & ~sf; sm = m & sf
    vol = (err * vm.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vm.sum().clamp(min=1)
    surf = (err * sm.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / sm.sum().clamp(min=1)
    loss = vol + 10.0 * surf
    opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    n_done += 1
    if n_done == 25:
        break
torch.cuda.synchronize()
dt = time.time() - t0
print(f'25 steps (bs={bs}): {dt:.1f}s -> {dt/25*1000:.0f} ms/step -> ~{dt/25*len(loader):.0f}s/epoch  ({len(loader)} steps/epoch)')
print(f'peak GB: {torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated()/1e9:.1f}')
EOF

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/train.py", "content": """"Train a Transolver surrogate on TandemFoilSet.

Four validation tracks with one pinned test track each:
  val_single_in_dist      / test_single_in_dist      — in-distribution sanity
  val_geom_camber_rc      / test_geom_camber_rc      — unseen front foil (raceCar)
  val_geom_camber_cruise  / test_geom_camber_cruise  — unseen front foil (cruise)
  val_re_rand             / test_re_rand             — stratified Re holdout

Primary ranking metric is ``avg/mae_surf_p`` — the equal-weight mean surface
pressure MAE across the four splits, computed in the original (denormalized)
target space. Train/val/test MAE all flow through ``data.scoring`` so the
numbers are produced identically.

This v2 trainer is a parameterizable upgrade of the original baseline:

  * Larger Transolver capacity (n_hidden, n_layers, n_head, slice_num CLI flags)
    matching the AirfRANS settings used in the Transolver paper (thuml/Transolver
    Airfoil-Design-AirfRANS/main.py): n_hidden=256, n_layers=8, n_head=8,
    slice_num=32 (Wu et al., ICML 2024 — arxiv 2402.02366).
  * Optional Transolver++ "Ada-Temp" per-point temperature inside
    PhysicsAttention (Luo et al., ICML 2025 — arxiv 2502.02414, §4.1).
  * Optional Gumbel-Softmax slice reparameterization (Rep-Slice, same paper).
  * Mixed precision (bf16 autocast) for ~2× throughput on the RTX PRO 6000s.
  * Loss choice: mse | huber | l1 (paper uses MSE; metric is MAE → Huber/L1
    align gradients with evaluation).
  * Optional EMA model evaluated at val / test time.
  * OneCycleLR with cosine annealing and configurable warmup (paper recipe).

The model contract (input/output shapes, normalization, per-split scoring) and
``data/`` interfaces are unchanged from the original ``train.py``.

Usage:
  python train.py [--debug] [--epochs 50] [--agent <name>] [--wandb_name <name>]
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import math
import os
import subprocess
import time
from copy import deepcopy
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from pathlib import Path

import simple_parsing as sp
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
import wandb
import yaml
from einops import rearrange
from timm.layers import trunc_normal_
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
from tqdm import tqdm

from data import (
    TEST_SPLIT_NAMES,
    VAL_SPLIT_NAMES,
    X_DIM,
    accumulate_batch,
    aggregate_splits,
    finalize_split,
    load_data,
    load_test_data,
    pad_collate,
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Transolver model
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ACTIVATION = {
    "gelu": nn.GELU,
    "tanh": nn.Tanh,
    "sigmoid": nn.Sigmoid,
    "relu": nn.ReLU,
    "leaky_relu": nn.LeakyReLU(0.1),
    "softplus": nn.Softplus,
    "ELU": nn.ELU,
    "silu": nn.SiLU,
}


class MLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_input, n_hidden, n_output, n_layers=1, act="gelu", res=True):
        super().__init__()
        act_fn = ACTIVATION[act]
        self.n_layers = n_layers
        self.res = res
        self.linear_pre = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_input, n_hidden), act_fn())
        self.linear_post = nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_output)
        self.linears = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(n_hidden, n_hidden), act_fn()) for _ in range(n_layers)]
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear_pre(x)
        for i in range(self.n_layers):
            x = self.linears[i](x) + x if self.res else self.linears[i](x)
        return self.linear_post(x)


class PhysicsAttention(nn.Module):
    """Physics-aware attention for irregular meshes.

    Optional Transolver++ extensions (Luo et al., ICML 2025):
      * ``ada_temp``: per-point temperature τ(x) added to the global parameter,
        sharpens slice assignments locally (Eq. 5 in arxiv 2502.02414).
      * ``gumbel``:   Gumbel-softmax reparameterization during training to make
        slice assignment near-discrete (Eq. 6, "Rep-Slice").
    """

    def __init__(self, dim, heads=8, dim_head=64, dropout=0.0, slice_num=64,
                 ada_temp: bool = False, gumbel: bool = False):
        super().__init__()
        inner_dim = dim_head * heads
        self.dim_head = dim_head
        self.heads = heads
        self.softmax = nn.Softmax(dim=-1)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)
        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
        self.ada_temp = ada_temp
        self.gumbel = gumbel
        if ada_temp:
            # Per-point temperature offset, predicted from per-head x_mid.
            self.ada_temp_proj = nn.Linear(dim_head, 1)

        self.in_project_x = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
        self.in_project_fx = nn.Linear(dim, inner_dim)
        self.in_project_slice = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)
        torch.nn.init.orthogonal_(self.in_project_slice.weight)
        self.to_q = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_v = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_out = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(inner_dim, dim), nn.Dropout(dropout))

    def _slice_weights(self, x_mid: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # x_mid: [B, H, N, dim_head]
        if self.ada_temp:
            tau = self.temperature + F.softplus(self.ada_temp_proj(x_mid))  # [B,H,N,1]
        else:
            tau = self.temperature
        logits = self.in_project_slice(x_mid) / tau  # [B,H,N,M]
        if self.gumbel and self.training:
            # Sample standard Gumbel noise.
            eps = torch.rand_like(logits).clamp_min(1e-9)
            gumbel = -torch.log(-torch.log(eps).clamp_min(1e-9))
            return self.softmax((logits + gumbel))
        return self.softmax(logits)

    def forward(self, x):
        B, N, _ = x.shape

        fx_mid = (
            self.in_project_fx(x)
            .reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head)
            .permute(0, 2, 1, 3)
            .contiguous()
        )
        x_mid = (
            self.in_project_x(x)
            .reshape(B, N, self.heads, self.dim_head)
            .permute(0, 2, 1, 3)
            .contiguous()
        )
        slice_weights = self._slice_weights(x_mid)
        slice_norm = slice_weights.sum(2)
        slice_token = torch.einsum("bhnc,bhng->bhgc", fx_mid, slice_weights)
        slice_token = slice_token / ((slice_norm + 1e-5)[:, :, :, None].repeat(1, 1, 1, self.dim_head))

        q = self.to_q(slice_token)
        k = self.to_k(slice_token)
        v = self.to_v(slice_token)
        out_slice = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
            q, k, v,
            dropout_p=self.dropout.p if self.training else 0.0,
            is_causal=False,
        )

        out_x = torch.einsum("bhgc,bhng->bhnc", out_slice, slice_weights)
        out_x = rearrange(out_x, "b h n d -> b n (h d)")
        return self.to_out(out_x)


class TransolverBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, num_heads, hidden_dim, dropout, act="gelu",
                 mlp_ratio=4, last_layer=False, out_dim=1, slice_num=32,
                 ada_temp: bool = False, gumbel: bool = False):
        super().__init__()
        self.last_layer = last_layer
        self.ln_1 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.attn = PhysicsAttention(
            hidden_dim, heads=num_heads, dim_head=hidden_dim // num_heads,
            dropout=dropout, slice_num=slice_num,
            ada_temp=ada_temp, gumbel=gumbel,
        )
        self.ln_2 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
        self.mlp = MLP(hidden_dim, hidden_dim * mlp_ratio, hidden_dim,
                       n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        if self.last_layer:
            self.ln_3 = nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim)
            self.mlp2 = nn.Sequential(
                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, hidden_dim), nn.GELU(),
                nn.Linear(hidden_dim, out_dim),
            )

    def forward(self, fx):
        fx = self.attn(self.ln_1(fx)) + fx
        fx = self.mlp(self.ln_2(fx)) + fx
        if self.last_layer:
            return self.mlp2(self.ln_3(fx))
        return fx


class Transolver(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, space_dim=1, n_layers=5, n_hidden=256, dropout=0.0,
                 n_head=8, act="gelu", mlp_ratio=1, fun_dim=1, out_dim=1,
                 slice_num=32, ref=8, unified_pos=False,
                 ada_temp: bool = False, gumbel: bool = False,
                 output_fields: list[str] | None = None,
                 output_dims: list[int] | None = None):
        super().__init__()
        self.ref = ref
        self.unified_pos = unified_pos
        self.output_fields = output_fields or []
        self.output_dims = output_dims or []

        if self.unified_pos:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + ref**3, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)
        else:
            self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden,
                                  n_layers=0, res=False, act=act)

        self.n_hidden = n_hidden
        self.space_dim = space_dim
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
            TransolverBlock(
                num_heads=n_head, hidden_dim=n_hidden, dropout=dropout,
                act=act, mlp_ratio=mlp_ratio, out_dim=out_dim,
                slice_num=slice_num, last_layer=(i == n_layers - 1),
                ada_temp=ada_temp, gumbel=gumbel,
            )
            for i in range(n_layers)
        ])
        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
        self.apply(self._init_weights)

    def _init_weights(self, m):
        if isinstance(m, nn.Linear):
            trunc_normal_(m.weight, std=0.02)
            if m.bias is not None:
                nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
        elif isinstance(m, (nn.LayerNorm, nn.BatchNorm1d)):
            nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0)
            nn.init.constant_(m.weight, 1.0)

    def forward(self, data, **kwargs):
        x = data["x"]
        fx = self.preprocess(x) + self.placeholder[None, None, :]
        for block in self.blocks:
            fx = block(fx)
        return {"preds": fx}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Loss helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _per_node_residual(loss: str, pred: torch.Tensor, target: torch.Tensor,
                       huber_delta: float) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Return per-node, per-channel residual contribution (same shape as ``pred``)."""
    if loss == "mse":
        return (pred - target) ** 2
    if loss == "l1":
        return (pred - target).abs()
    if loss == "huber":
        diff = (pred - target).abs()
        quad = 0.5 * diff * diff / huber_delta
        lin = diff - 0.5 * huber_delta
        return torch.where(diff <= huber_delta, quad, lin)
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown loss: {loss}")


def compute_loss(pred: torch.Tensor, y_norm: torch.Tensor,
                 is_surface: torch.Tensor, mask: torch.Tensor,
                 surf_weight: float, p_surf_extra_weight: float,
                 loss_type: str, huber_delta: float
                 ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    """Masked vol/surf loss with optional extra surface-pressure term.

    ``pred`` and ``y_norm`` are in normalized output space, shapes ``[B,N,3]``.
    Returns ``(total, vol_loss, surf_loss)`` for logging.
    """
    res = _per_node_residual(loss_type, pred, y_norm, huber_delta)  # [B,N,3]
    vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface  # [B,N]
    surf_mask = mask & is_surface
    vol_count = vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
    surf_count = surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
    vol_loss = (res * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / vol_count
    surf_loss = (res * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum() / surf_count
    total = vol_loss + surf_weight * surf_loss
    if p_surf_extra_weight > 0:
        surf_p_loss = (res[..., 2] * surf_mask).sum() / surf_count
        total = total + p_surf_extra_weight * surf_p_loss
    return total, vol_loss, surf_loss


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Evaluation helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, surf_weight, device, amp_dtype) -> dict[str, float]:
    """Run inference over a split and return metrics matching the organizer scorer.

    ``loss`` is the normalized-space MSE used for training monitoring (kept
    independent of the training loss type so val curves stay comparable across
    runs); the MAE channels are in the original target space and accumulated
    per organizer ``score.py`` (float64, non-finite samples skipped).
    """
    vol_loss_sum = surf_loss_sum = 0.0
    mae_surf = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    mae_vol = torch.zeros(3, dtype=torch.float64, device=device)
    n_surf = n_vol = n_batches = 0

    use_amp = amp_dtype is not None
    autocast_ctx = torch.amp.autocast("cuda", dtype=amp_dtype) if use_amp \
        else _NullContext()

    with torch.no_grad():
        for x, y, is_surface, mask in loader:
            x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
            mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

            x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
            y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
            with autocast_ctx:
                pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
            pred = pred.float()  # downcast back to fp32 for accuracy in scoring

            sq_err = (pred - y_norm) ** 2
            vol_mask = mask & ~is_surface
            surf_mask = mask & is_surface
            vol_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * vol_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / vol_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            surf_loss_sum += (
                (sq_err * surf_mask.unsqueeze(-1)).sum()
                / surf_mask.sum().clamp(min=1)
            ).item()
            n_batches += 1

            pred_orig = pred * stats["y_std"] + stats["y_mean"]
            ds, dv = accumulate_batch(pred_orig, y, is_surface, mask, mae_surf, mae_vol)
            n_surf += ds
            n_vol += dv

    vol_loss = vol_loss_sum / max(n_batches, 1)
    surf_loss = surf_loss_sum / max(n_batches, 1)
    out = {"vol_loss": vol_loss, "surf_loss": surf_loss,
           "loss": vol_loss + surf_weight * surf_loss}
    out.update(finalize_split(mae_surf, mae_vol, n_surf, n_vol))
    return out


class _NullContext:
    """Replacement for ``contextlib.nullcontext()`` to avoid the import."""
    def __enter__(self):
        return None

    def __exit__(self, *args):
        return False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EMA
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class EMA:
    """Exponential moving average of model parameters.

    Keeps a CPU-pinned shadow set; ``state_dict()`` returns the EMA tensors so
    they can be swapped in place via ``model.load_state_dict`` for evaluation.
    """

    def __init__(self, model: nn.Module, decay: float):
        self.decay = decay
        self.shadow: dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {
            k: v.detach().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()
        }

    @torch.no_grad()
    def update(self, model: nn.Module) -> None:
        for k, v in model.state_dict().items():
            if v.dtype.is_floating_point:
                self.shadow[k].mul_(self.decay).add_(v.detach(), alpha=1.0 - self.decay)
            else:
                self.shadow[k].copy_(v)

    def state_dict(self) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
        return {k: v.clone() for k, v in self.shadow.items()}


def _sanitize_artifact_token(s: str) -> str:
    """wandb artifact names allow alnum, '-', '_', '.' — replace everything else."""
    out = "".join(c if c.isalnum() or c in "-_." else "-" for c in s)
    return out.strip("-_.") or "run"


def _git_commit_short() -> str:
    try:
        return subprocess.check_output(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"],
            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, text=True,
        ).strip() or "unknown"
    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError):
        return "unknown"


def save_model_artifact(
    run,
    model_path: Path,
    model_dir: Path,
    cfg: "Config",
    best_metrics: dict,
    best_avg_surf_p: float,
    test_metrics: dict | None,
    test_avg: dict | None,
    n_params: int,
    model_config: dict,
) -> None:
    """Log the best checkpoint as a wandb model artifact."""
    if cfg.wandb_name:
        base = _sanitize_artifact_token(cfg.wandb_name)
    elif cfg.agent:
        base = _sanitize_artifact_token(cfg.agent)
    else:
        base = "tandemfoil"
    artifact_name = f"model-{base}-{run.id}"

    metadata: dict = {
        "run_id": run.id,
        "run_name": run.name,
        "agent": cfg.agent,
        "wandb_name": cfg.wandb_name,
        "wandb_group": cfg.wandb_group,
        "git_commit": _git_commit_short(),
        "n_params": n_params,
        "model_config": model_config,
        "best_epoch": best_metrics["epoch"],
        "best_val_avg/mae_surf_p": best_avg_surf_p,
        "lr": cfg.lr,
        "weight_decay": cfg.weight_decay,
        "batch_size": cfg.batch_size,
        "surf_weight": cfg.surf_weight,
        "epochs_configured": cfg.epochs,
    }

    description = (
        f"Transolver checkpoint — best val_avg/mae_surf_p = {best_avg_surf_p:.4f} "
        f"at epoch {best_metrics['epoch']}"
    )

    if test_avg is not None and "avg/mae_surf_p" in test_avg:
        metadata["test_avg/mae_surf_p"] = test_avg["avg/mae_surf_p"]
        if test_metrics is not None:
            for split_name, m in test_metrics.items():
                metadata[f"test/{split_name}/mae_surf_p"] = m["mae_surf_p"]
        description += f" | test_avg/mae_surf_p = {test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}"

    artifact = wandb.Artifact(
        name=artifact_name,
        type="model",
        description=description,
        metadata=metadata,
    )
    artifact.add_file(str(model_path), name="checkpoint.pt")
    config_yaml = model_dir / "config.yaml"
    if config_yaml.exists():
        artifact.add_file(str(config_yaml), name="config.yaml")

    aliases = ["best", f"epoch-{best_metrics['epoch']}"]
    run.log_artifact(artifact, aliases=aliases)
    print(f"\nLogged model artifact '{artifact_name}' (aliases: {', '.join(aliases)})")


def print_split_metrics(split_name: str, m: dict[str, float]) -> None:
    print(
        f"    {split_name:<26s} "
        f"loss={m['loss']:.4f}  "
        f"surf[p={m['mae_surf_p']:.4f} Ux={m['mae_surf_Ux']:.4f} Uy={m['mae_surf_Uy']:.4f}]  "
        f"vol[p={m['mae_vol_p']:.4f} Ux={m['mae_vol_Ux']:.4f} Uy={m['mae_vol_Uy']:.4f}]"
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Training
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MIN = float(os.environ.get("SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES", "30"))


@dataclass
class Config:
    # Optimization
    lr: float = 5e-4
    weight_decay: float = 1e-4
    batch_size: int = 4
    epochs: int = 50
    scheduler: str = "cosine"        # cosine | onecycle
    warmup_pct: float = 0.0          # for onecycle, fraction of total steps

    # Loss
    loss: str = "mse"                # mse | huber | l1
    huber_delta: float = 1.0
    surf_weight: float = 10.0
    p_surf_extra_weight: float = 0.0

    # Mixed precision
    amp: str = "off"                 # off | bf16 | fp16

    # EMA
    ema: bool = False
    ema_decay: float = 0.999

    # Model architecture
    n_hidden: int = 128
    n_layers: int = 5
    n_head: int = 4
    slice_num: int = 64
    mlp_ratio: int = 2
    dropout: float = 0.0
    unified_pos: bool = False
    ref: int = 8
    ada_temp: bool = False
    gumbel: bool = False

    # Run plumbing
    splits_dir: str = "/mnt/new-pvc/datasets/tandemfoil/splits_v2"
    wandb_group: str | None = None
    wandb_name: str | None = None
    agent: str | None = None
    debug: bool = False
    skip_test: bool = False
    seed: int = 0


cfg = sp.parse(Config)
MAX_EPOCHS = 3 if cfg.debug else cfg.epochs
MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MIN

# Reproducibility (within reason for non-deterministic CUDA kernels).
torch.manual_seed(cfg.seed)
torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(cfg.seed)

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Device: {device}" + (" [DEBUG]" if cfg.debug else ""))

amp_dtype = {"off": None, "bf16": torch.bfloat16, "fp16": torch.float16}[cfg.amp]
print(f"AMP: {cfg.amp}  loss: {cfg.loss}  ema: {cfg.ema}@{cfg.ema_decay}")

train_ds, val_splits, stats, sample_weights = load_data(cfg.splits_dir, debug=cfg.debug)
stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}

loader_kwargs = dict(collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                     persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)

if cfg.debug:
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size,
                              shuffle=True, **loader_kwargs)
else:
    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True)
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size,
                              sampler=sampler, **loader_kwargs)

val_loaders = {
    name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
    for name, ds in val_splits.items()
}

model_config = dict(
    space_dim=2,
    fun_dim=X_DIM - 2,
    out_dim=3,
    n_hidden=cfg.n_hidden,
    n_layers=cfg.n_layers,
    n_head=cfg.n_head,
    slice_num=cfg.slice_num,
    mlp_ratio=cfg.mlp_ratio,
    dropout=cfg.dropout,
    unified_pos=cfg.unified_pos,
    ref=cfg.ref,
    ada_temp=cfg.ada_temp,
    gumbel=cfg.gumbel,
    output_fields=["Ux", "Uy", "p"],
    output_dims=[1, 1, 1],
)

model = Transolver(**model_config).to(device)
n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
print(f"Model: Transolver ({n_params/1e6:.2f}M params)  "
      f"hidden={cfg.n_hidden} layers={cfg.n_layers} heads={cfg.n_head} slice={cfg.slice_num}  "
      f"unified_pos={cfg.unified_pos} ada_temp={cfg.ada_temp} gumbel={cfg.gumbel}")

ema = EMA(model, decay=cfg.ema_decay) if cfg.ema else None

optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=cfg.lr, weight_decay=cfg.weight_decay)
if cfg.scheduler == "cosine":
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=MAX_EPOCHS)
    step_per_step = False
elif cfg.scheduler == "onecycle":
    steps_per_epoch = max(1, len(train_loader))
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=cfg.lr,
        epochs=MAX_EPOCHS,
        steps_per_epoch=steps_per_epoch,
        pct_start=cfg.warmup_pct if cfg.warmup_pct > 0 else 0.05,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=25.0,
        final_div_factor=1e4,
    )
    step_per_step = True
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown scheduler {cfg.scheduler}")

run = wandb.init(
    entity=os.environ.get("WANDB_ENTITY"),
    project=os.environ.get("WANDB_PROJECT"),
    group=cfg.wandb_group,
    name=cfg.wandb_name,
    tags=[cfg.agent] if cfg.agent else [],
    config={
        **asdict(cfg),
        "model_config": model_config,
        "n_params": n_params,
        "train_samples": len(train_ds),
        "val_samples": {k: len(v) for k, v in val_splits.items()},
    },
    mode=os.environ.get("WANDB_MODE", "online"),
)

wandb.define_metric("global_step")
wandb.define_metric("train/*", step_metric="global_step")
wandb.define_metric("val/*", step_metric="global_step")
for _name in VAL_SPLIT_NAMES:
    wandb.define_metric(f"{_name}/*", step_metric="global_step")
wandb.define_metric("lr", step_metric="global_step")

model_dir = Path(f"models/model-{run.id}")
model_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
model_path = model_dir / "checkpoint.pt"
with open(model_dir / "config.yaml", "w") as f:
    yaml.dump(model_config, f)

best_avg_surf_p = float("inf")
best_metrics: dict = {}
global_step = 0
train_start = time.time()


def _eval_set(state_source) -> tuple[dict[str, dict[str, float]], dict[str, float]]:
    """Run validation with the given state_source (model or EMA)."""
    if state_source is ema:
        # Swap EMA weights into the model for evaluation, then restore.
        backup = {k: v.detach().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()}
        model.load_state_dict(ema.state_dict())
    else:
        backup = None
    try:
        per = {
            name: evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, cfg.surf_weight, device, amp_dtype)
            for name, loader in val_loaders.items()
        }
    finally:
        if backup is not None:
            model.load_state_dict(backup)
    avg = aggregate_splits(per)
    return per, avg


for epoch in range(MAX_EPOCHS):
    if (time.time() - train_start) / 60.0 >= MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN:
        print(f"Timeout ({MAX_TIMEOUT_MIN} min). Stopping.")
        break

    t0 = time.time()
    model.train()
    epoch_vol = epoch_surf = 0.0
    n_batches = 0

    autocast_ctx = torch.amp.autocast("cuda", dtype=amp_dtype) if amp_dtype is not None \
        else _NullContext()
    scaler = torch.amp.GradScaler("cuda") if amp_dtype is torch.float16 else None

    for x, y, is_surface, mask in tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{MAX_EPOCHS}", leave=False):
        x = x.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        y = y.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        is_surface = is_surface.to(device, non_blocking=True)
        mask = mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)

        x_norm = (x - stats["x_mean"]) / stats["x_std"]
        y_norm = (y - stats["y_mean"]) / stats["y_std"]
        with autocast_ctx:
            pred = model({"x": x_norm})["preds"]
            loss, vol_loss, surf_loss = compute_loss(
                pred, y_norm, is_surface, mask,
                cfg.surf_weight, cfg.p_surf_extra_weight,
                cfg.loss, cfg.huber_delta,
            )

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        if scaler is not None:
            scaler.scale(loss).backward()
            scaler.step(optimizer)
            scaler.update()
        else:
            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()
        if step_per_step:
            scheduler.step()
        if ema is not None:
            ema.update(model)

        global_step += 1
        wandb.log({"train/loss": loss.item(), "global_step": global_step})

        epoch_vol += vol_loss.item()
        epoch_surf += surf_loss.item()
        n_batches += 1

    if not step_per_step:
        scheduler.step()
    epoch_vol /= max(n_batches, 1)
    epoch_surf /= max(n_batches, 1)

    # --- Validate (EMA weights if enabled, else live model) ---
    model.eval()
    eval_source = ema if ema is not None else model
    split_metrics, val_avg = _eval_set(eval_source)
    avg_surf_p = val_avg["avg/mae_surf_p"]
    val_loss_mean = sum(m["loss"] for m in split_metrics.values()) / len(split_metrics)
    dt = time.time() - t0

    log_metrics = {
        "train/vol_loss": epoch_vol,
        "train/surf_loss": epoch_surf,
        "val/loss": val_loss_mean,
        "lr": scheduler.get_last_lr()[0] if hasattr(scheduler, "get_last_lr") else cfg.lr,
        "epoch_time_s": dt,
        "global_step": global_step,
    }
    for split_name, m in split_metrics.items():
        for k, v in m.items():
            log_metrics[f"{split_name}/{k}"] = v
    for k, v in val_avg.items():
        log_metrics[f"val_{k}"] = v  # val_avg/mae_surf_p etc.
    wandb.log(log_metrics)

    tag = ""
    if avg_surf_p < best_avg_surf_p:
        best_avg_surf_p = avg_surf_p
        best_metrics = {
            "epoch": epoch + 1,
            "val_avg/mae_surf_p": avg_surf_p,
            "per_split": split_metrics,
        }
        # Save EMA weights if EMA is on, else live model.
        if ema is not None:
            torch.save(ema.state_dict(), model_path)
        else:
            torch.save(model.state_dict(), model_path)
        tag = " *"

    peak_gb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1e9 if torch.cuda.is_available() else 0.0
    print(
        f"Epoch {epoch+1:3d} ({dt:.0f}s) [{peak_gb:.1f}GB]  "
        f"train[vol={epoch_vol:.4f} surf={epoch_surf:.4f}]  "
        f"val_avg_surf_p={avg_surf_p:.4f}{tag}"
    )
    for name in VAL_SPLIT_NAMES:
        print_split_metrics(name, split_metrics[name])

total_time = (time.time() - train_start) / 60.0
print(f"\nTraining done in {total_time:.1f} min")

# --- Test evaluation + artifact upload ---
if best_metrics:
    print(f"\nBest val: epoch {best_metrics['epoch']}, val_avg/mae_surf_p = {best_avg_surf_p:.4f}")
    wandb.summary.update({
        "best_epoch": best_metrics["epoch"],
        "best_val_avg/mae_surf_p": best_avg_surf_p,
        "total_train_minutes": total_time,
    })

    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(model_path, map_location=device, weights_only=True))
    model.eval()

    test_metrics = None
    test_avg = None
    if not cfg.skip_test:
        print("\nEvaluating on held-out test splits...")
        test_datasets = load_test_data(cfg.splits_dir, debug=cfg.debug)
        test_loaders = {
            name: DataLoader(ds, batch_size=cfg.batch_size, shuffle=False, **loader_kwargs)
            for name, ds in test_datasets.items()
        }
        test_metrics = {
            name: evaluate_split(model, loader, stats, cfg.surf_weight, device, amp_dtype)
            for name, loader in test_loaders.items()
        }
        test_avg = aggregate_splits(test_metrics)
        print(f"\n  TEST  avg_surf_p={test_avg['avg/mae_surf_p']:.4f}")
        for name in TEST_SPLIT_NAMES:
            print_split_metrics(name, test_metrics[name])

        test_log: dict[str, float] = {}
        for split_name, m in test_metrics.items():
            for k, v in m.items():
                test_log[f"test/{split_name}/{k}"] = v
        for k, v in test_avg.items():
            test_log[f"test_{k}"] = v
        wandb.log(test_log)
        wandb.summary.update(test_log)

    save_model_artifact(
        run=run,
        model_path=model_path,
        model_dir=model_dir,
        cfg=cfg,
        best_metrics=best_metrics,
        best_avg_surf_p=best_avg_surf_p,
        test_metrics=test_metrics,
        test_avg=test_avg,
        n_params=n_params,
        model_config=model_config,
    )
else:
    print("\nNo checkpoint was saved (no epoch improved on val_avg/mae_surf_p). Skipping artifact upload.")

wandb.finish()
"}

{"command": "cat > /tmp/time_real.py <<'EOF'
"""Standalone timing — does NOT import train.py (which would re-run training)."""
import sys, time, os
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target')

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
from einops import rearrange
from timm.layers import trunc_normal_
from data import load_data, pad_collate, X_DIM

device = torch.device('cuda')

# Inline minimal Transolver — same as in train.py — to avoid running its top-level code.
class MLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ni, nh, no, n_layers=0, act='gelu', res=False):
        super().__init__()
        af = nn.GELU if act == 'gelu' else nn.ReLU
        self.pre = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(ni, nh), af())
        self.post = nn.Linear(nh, no)
        self.layers = nn.ModuleList(
            [nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(nh, nh), af()) for _ in range(n_layers)]
        )
        self.res = res
    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.pre(x)
        for L in self.layers:
            x = L(x) + x if self.res else L(x)
        return self.post(x)

class PA(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num):
        super().__init__()
        self.heads = heads; self.dim_head = dim_head
        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
        self.in_x = nn.Linear(dim, heads * dim_head)
        self.in_fx = nn.Linear(dim, heads * dim_head)
        self.in_slice = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)
        torch.nn.init.orthogonal_(self.in_slice.weight)
        self.to_q = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_v = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_out = nn.Linear(heads * dim_head, dim)
    def forward(self, x):
        B, N, _ = x.shape
        H, D = self.heads, self.dim_head
        fx = self.in_fx(x).reshape(B, N, H, D).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        xm = self.in_x(x).reshape(B, N, H, D).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        sw = F.softmax(self.in_slice(xm) / self.temperature, dim=-1)
        sn = sw.sum(2)
        st = torch.einsum('bhnc,bhng->bhgc', fx, sw)
        st = st / ((sn + 1e-5)[:, :, :, None].repeat(1, 1, 1, D))
        q, k, v = self.to_q(st), self.to_k(st), self.to_v(st)
        out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False)
        ox = torch.einsum('bhgc,bhng->bhnc', out, sw)
        return self.to_out(rearrange(ox, 'b h n d -> b n (h d)'))

class Block(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio, last=False, out_dim=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.last = last
        self.ln1 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
        self.attn = PA(dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num)
        self.ln2 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
        self.mlp = MLP(dim, dim * mlp_ratio, dim, n_layers=0, res=False)
        if last:
            self.ln3 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
            self.head = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(dim, dim), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(dim, out_dim))
    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.attn(self.ln1(x)) + x
        x = self.mlp(self.ln2(x)) + x
        return self.head(self.ln3(x)) if self.last else x

class Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, fun_dim, space_dim, n_hidden, n_layers, n_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio, out_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden, n_layers=0, res=False)
        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
            Block(n_hidden, n_head, n_hidden // n_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio,
                  last=(i == n_layers - 1), out_dim=out_dim)
            for i in range(n_layers)
        ])
    def forward(self, x):
        fx = self.preprocess(x) + self.placeholder[None, None, :]
        for b in self.blocks:
            fx = b(fx)
        return fx

def time_config(name, n_hidden, n_layers, n_head, slice_num, batch_size, amp_dtype, n_steps=15):
    torch.cuda.empty_cache()
    train_ds, _, stats, sw = load_data()
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sw, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True)
    loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=batch_size, sampler=sampler,
                        collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                        persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
    model = Net(fun_dim=X_DIM-2, space_dim=2, n_hidden=n_hidden, n_layers=n_layers,
                n_head=n_head, slice_num=slice_num, mlp_ratio=2, out_dim=3).to(device)
    nparams = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()) / 1e6
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
    autocast = torch.amp.autocast('cuda', dtype=amp_dtype) if amp_dtype else None
    # Warm up
    it = iter(loader)
    for _ in range(2):
        x, y, sf, m = next(it)
        x, y, sf, m = [t.to(device, non_blocking=True) for t in (x, y, sf, m)]
        xn = (x - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
        yn = (y - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
        if autocast:
            with autocast: pred = model(xn); err = (pred - yn) ** 2; loss = err.mean()
        else:
            pred = model(xn); err = (pred - yn) ** 2; loss = err.mean()
        opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
    t0 = time.time()
    n = 0
    for _ in range(n_steps):
        try:
            x, y, sf, m = next(it)
        except StopIteration:
            it = iter(loader); x, y, sf, m = next(it)
        x, y, sf, m = [t.to(device, non_blocking=True) for t in (x, y, sf, m)]
        xn = (x - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
        yn = (y - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
        if autocast:
            with autocast: pred = model(xn); err = (pred - yn) ** 2; loss = err.mean()
        else:
            pred = model(xn); err = (pred - yn) ** 2; loss = err.mean()
        opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
        n += 1
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
    dt = time.time() - t0
    steps_per_epoch = len(loader)
    epoch_s = dt / n * steps_per_epoch
    peak_gb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1e9
    print(f'{name:30s}  {nparams:5.1f}M params  {dt/n*1000:6.0f} ms/step  ~{epoch_s:5.0f}s/epoch ({steps_per_epoch} st/ep)  peak={peak_gb:.1f}GB')
    return epoch_s

print('Timing on real data, GPU 0...')
time_config('baseline (h128 l5 h4 sn64 fp32)', 128, 5, 4, 64, 4, None)

"""Timing run 2 — focus on bf16 + larger models, varying batch size."""
import sys, time, os
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target')

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
from einops import rearrange
from data import load_data, pad_collate, X_DIM

device = torch.device('cuda')

class MLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, ni, nh, no):
        super().__init__()
        self.l = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(ni, nh), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(nh, no))
    def forward(self, x): return self.l(x)

class PA(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num):
        super().__init__()
        self.heads = heads; self.dim_head = dim_head
        self.temperature = nn.Parameter(torch.ones([1, heads, 1, 1]) * 0.5)
        self.in_x = nn.Linear(dim, heads * dim_head)
        self.in_fx = nn.Linear(dim, heads * dim_head)
        self.in_slice = nn.Linear(dim_head, slice_num)
        torch.nn.init.orthogonal_(self.in_slice.weight)
        self.to_q = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_k = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_v = nn.Linear(dim_head, dim_head, bias=False)
        self.to_out = nn.Linear(heads * dim_head, dim)
    def forward(self, x):
        B, N, _ = x.shape
        H, D = self.heads, self.dim_head
        fx = self.in_fx(x).reshape(B, N, H, D).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        xm = self.in_x(x).reshape(B, N, H, D).permute(0, 2, 1, 3).contiguous()
        sw = F.softmax(self.in_slice(xm) / self.temperature, dim=-1)
        sn = sw.sum(2)
        st = torch.einsum('bhnc,bhng->bhgc', fx, sw)
        st = st / ((sn + 1e-5)[:, :, :, None].repeat(1, 1, 1, D))
        q, k, v = self.to_q(st), self.to_k(st), self.to_v(st)
        out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False)
        ox = torch.einsum('bhgc,bhng->bhnc', out, sw)
        return self.to_out(rearrange(ox, 'b h n d -> b n (h d)'))

class Block(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio, last=False, out_dim=1):
        super().__init__()
        self.last = last
        self.ln1 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
        self.attn = PA(dim, heads, dim_head, slice_num)
        self.ln2 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
        self.mlp = MLP(dim, dim * mlp_ratio, dim)
        if last:
            self.ln3 = nn.LayerNorm(dim)
            self.head = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(dim, dim), nn.GELU(), nn.Linear(dim, out_dim))
    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.attn(self.ln1(x)) + x
        x = self.mlp(self.ln2(x)) + x
        return self.head(self.ln3(x)) if self.last else x

class Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, fun_dim, space_dim, n_hidden, n_layers, n_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio, out_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.preprocess = MLP(fun_dim + space_dim, n_hidden * 2, n_hidden)
        self.placeholder = nn.Parameter((1 / n_hidden) * torch.rand(n_hidden))
        self.blocks = nn.ModuleList([
            Block(n_hidden, n_head, n_hidden // n_head, slice_num, mlp_ratio,
                  last=(i == n_layers - 1), out_dim=out_dim)
            for i in range(n_layers)
        ])
    def forward(self, x):
        fx = self.preprocess(x) + self.placeholder[None, None, :]
        for b in self.blocks:
            fx = b(fx)
        return fx

def time_config(name, n_hidden, n_layers, n_head, slice_num, batch_size, amp_dtype, n_steps=12):
    torch.cuda.empty_cache(); torch.cuda.reset_peak_memory_stats()
    train_ds, _, stats, sw = load_data()
    stats = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in stats.items()}
    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sw, num_samples=len(train_ds), replacement=True)
    loader = DataLoader(train_ds, batch_size=batch_size, sampler=sampler,
                        collate_fn=pad_collate, num_workers=4, pin_memory=True,
                        persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2)
    model = Net(fun_dim=X_DIM-2, space_dim=2, n_hidden=n_hidden, n_layers=n_layers,
                n_head=n_head, slice_num=slice_num, mlp_ratio=2, out_dim=3).to(device)
    nparams = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()) / 1e6
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3, weight_decay=1e-4)
    autocast = torch.amp.autocast('cuda', dtype=amp_dtype) if amp_dtype else None
    try:
        # Warm up
        it = iter(loader)
        for _ in range(2):
            x, y, sf, m = next(it)
            x, y, sf, m = [t.to(device, non_blocking=True) for t in (x, y, sf, m)]
            xn = (x - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
            yn = (y - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
            if autocast:
                with autocast:
                    pred = model(xn)
                    loss = ((pred - yn) ** 2).mean()
            else:
                pred = model(xn); loss = ((pred - yn) ** 2).mean()
            opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
        torch.cuda.synchronize()
        t0 = time.time()
        n = 0
        for _ in range(n_steps):
            try: x, y, sf, m = next(it)
            except StopIteration: it = iter(loader); x, y, sf, m = next(it)
            x, y, sf, m = [t.to(device, non_blocking=True) for t in (x, y, sf, m)]
            xn = (x - stats['x_mean']) / stats['x_std']
            yn = (y - stats['y_mean']) / stats['y_std']
            if autocast:
                with autocast:
                    pred = model(xn)
                    loss = ((pred - yn) ** 2).mean()
            else:
                pred = model(xn); loss = ((pred - yn) ** 2).mean()
            opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()
            n += 1
        torch.cuda.synchronize()
        dt = time.time() - t0
        steps_per_epoch = len(loader)
        epoch_s = dt / n * steps_per_epoch
        peak_gb = torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() / 1e9
        print(f'{name:38s} bs={batch_size}  {nparams:5.1f}M  {dt/n*1000:6.0f} ms/step  ~{epoch_s:5.0f}s/epoch ({steps_per_epoch}s/e)  peak={peak_gb:.1f}GB')
    except torch.cuda.OutOfMemoryError as e:
        print(f'{name:38s} bs={batch_size}  OOM')
    except RuntimeError as e:
        if 'out of memory' in str(e).lower():
            print(f'{name:38s} bs={batch_size}  OOM')
        else: raise
    finally:
        del model
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

print('Timing on real data, GPU 0...')
# baseline reference (we already have these numbers)

    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=cfg.lr,
        epochs=MAX_EPOCHS,
        steps_per_epoch=steps_per_epoch,
        pct_start=cfg.warmup_pct if cfg.warmup_pct > 0 else 0.05,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=25.0,
        final_div_factor=1e4,
    )
    step_per_step = True
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown scheduler {cfg.scheduler}")", "new_str": "if cfg.scheduler == "cosine":
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=MAX_EPOCHS)
    step_per_step = False
elif cfg.scheduler == "onecycle":
    # Optimizer steps per epoch = ceil(num_batches / grad_accum)
    accum = max(1, cfg.grad_accum)
    steps_per_epoch = max(1, (len(train_loader) + accum - 1) // accum)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=cfg.lr,
        epochs=MAX_EPOCHS,
        steps_per_epoch=steps_per_epoch,
        pct_start=cfg.warmup_pct if cfg.warmup_pct > 0 else 0.05,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=25.0,
        final_div_factor=1e4,
    )
    step_per_step = True
else:
    raise ValueError(f"Unknown scheduler {cfg.scheduler}")"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/train.py", "old_str": "    save_model_artifact(
        run=run,
        model_path=model_path,
        model_dir=model_dir,
        cfg=cfg,
        best_metrics=best_metrics,
        best_avg_surf_p=best_avg_surf_p,
        test_metrics=test_metrics,
        test_avg=test_avg,
        n_params=n_params,
        model_config=model_config,
    )
else:
    print("\nNo checkpoint was saved (no epoch improved on val_avg/mae_surf_p). Skipping artifact upload.")

wandb.finish()", "new_str": "    save_model_artifact(
        run=run,
        model_path=model_path,
        model_dir=model_dir,
        cfg=cfg,
        best_metrics=best_metrics,
        best_avg_surf_p=best_avg_surf_p,
        test_metrics=test_metrics,
        test_avg=test_avg,
        n_params=n_params,
        model_config=model_config,
    )

    # Append a structured result row for the benchmark contract.
    try:
        import json as _json
        results_path = Path("research/MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl")
        results_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        row = {
            "run_id": run.id,
            "wandb_name": cfg.wandb_name,
            "wandb_group": cfg.wandb_group,
            "agent": cfg.agent,
            "n_params": n_params,
            "config": asdict(cfg),
            "model_config": {k: v for k, v in model_config.items()
                             if k not in ("output_fields", "output_dims")},
            "best_epoch": best_metrics["epoch"],
            "best_val_avg/mae_surf_p": best_avg_surf_p,
            "best_per_split_val": {
                name: {k: float(v) for k, v in m.items()}
                for name, m in best_metrics["per_split"].items()
            },
            "test_avg/mae_surf_p": (test_avg or {}).get("avg/mae_surf_p"),
            "test_per_split": {
                name: {k: float(v) for k, v in m.items()}
                for name, m in (test_metrics or {}).items()
            } if test_metrics else None,
            "test_avg": {k: float(v) for k, v in (test_avg or {}).items()} if test_avg else None,
            "total_train_minutes": total_time,
            "git_commit": _git_commit_short(),
        }
        with open(results_path, "a") as fh:
            fh.write(_json.dumps(row) + "\n")
        print(f"Wrote result row to {results_path}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Failed to write MLINTERN_RESULTS.jsonl row: {e}")
else:
    print("\nNo checkpoint was saved (no epoch improved on val_avg/mae_surf_p). Skipping artifact upload.")

wandb.finish()"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/launch_logs/launcher.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Helper: launch a training job on a specific GPU, record PID for clean shutdown.
#
# Usage:  bash launch_logs/launcher.sh <gpu_id> <wandb_short_name> <timeout_min> <flags...>
# Records PID and command to launch_logs/runs.tsv.

set -euo pipefail
GPU=$1; shift
NAME=$1; shift
TIMEOUT_MIN=$1; shift
FLAGS=( "$@" )

LOG_DIR="/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/launch_logs"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/${NAME}.log"
TS=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

cd /workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target

# Train.py is the single entrypoint; use the canonical command shape from prompt.md.
nohup env CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$GPU" SENPAI_TIMEOUT_MINUTES="$TIMEOUT_MIN" \
  python ./train.py --epochs 999 --agent ml-intern-r4 \
    --wandb_group mlintern-pai2-72h-v4-r4 \
    --wandb_name "mlintern-pai2-72h-v4-r4/$NAME" \
    "${FLAGS[@]}" \
    > "$LOG_FILE" 2>&1 &

PID=$!
printf '%s\tgpu=%s\tpid=%s\tname=%s\ttimeout_min=%s\tflags=%s\n' \
  "$TS" "$GPU" "$PID" "$NAME" "$TIMEOUT_MIN" "${FLAGS[*]}" \
  >> "$LOG_DIR/runs.tsv"
echo "$PID $LOG_FILE"
"}

function.arguments:
{"path": "/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/launch_logs/monitor.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
# Quick status across all running training runs.
LOG_DIR="/workspace/ml-intern-benchmark/target/launch_logs"

printf "%-40s  %-7s  %-12s  %-13s  %s\n" "RUN" "STATUS" "EPOCH" "VAL_SURF_P" "BEST"
printf -- '-%.0s' {1..100}; echo
for f in $LOG_DIR/*.log; do
  name=$(basename "$f" .log)
  # Find PID for this run
  pid=$(awk -v n="$name" '$0 ~ n {print $3}' $LOG_DIR/runs.tsv | tr -d 'pid=' | tail -1)
  if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then status="RUN"; else status="STOPPED"; fi

  # Latest epoch summary line:  Epoch  N (Ts)  ...  val_avg_surf_p=X
  last=$(grep -aE "^Epoch +[0-9]+ +\(" "$f" | tail -1)
  if [ -n "$last" ]; then
    ep=$(echo "$last" | sed -E 's/^Epoch +([0-9]+).*/\1/')
    val=$(echo "$last" | grep -oE 'val_avg_surf_p=[0-9.]+' | sed 's/val_avg_surf_p=//')
    star=$(echo "$last" | grep -oE '\*$' || echo "")
  else
    ep=""; val=""; star=""
  fi

  # Best val so far (lowest)
  best=$(grep -aoE 'val_avg_surf_p=[0-9.]+ \*' "$f" | sed 's/val_avg_surf_p=//;s/ \*//' | sort -n | head -1)

  printf "%-40s  %-7s  %-12s  %-13s  %s\n" \
    "$name" "$status" "${ep:-?}" "${val:-?}${star}" "${best:-?}"
done
echo ""
echo "GPU memory:"
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=index,memory.used --format=csv,noheader | head -10
"}
function.name: write
